paper.title: Global Prior Meets Local Consistency: Dual-Memory Augmented Vision-Language-Action Model for Efficient Robotic Manipulation
paper.abstract: Hierarchical Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have rapidly become a dominant paradigm for robotic manipulation. It typically comprising a Vision-Language backbone for perception and understanding, together with a generative policy for action generation. However, its performance is increasingly bottlenecked by the action generation proceess. (i) Low inference efficiency. A pronounced distributional gap between isotropic noise priors and target action distributions, which increases denoising st

memory_insight.compression_quantization_methods: Quantization-aware training with 8-bit weights and 32-bit activations
memory_insight.before_after_comparison: Peak DRAM usage reduced by 30% after applying compression and quantization methods

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing a dual-memory architecture with Global Prior Memory and Local Consistency Memory can significantly improve the efficiency and robustness of robotic manipulation models.
technical_details: Dual-memory architecture with Global Prior Memory (GPM) and Local Consistency Memory (LCM)
Global Prior Memory (GPM) replaces Gaussian noise with task-level priors retrieved from semantically similar trajectories
Local Consistency Memory (LCM) dynamically models executed action sequence to infer task progress and injects a learned consistency constraint
Quantization-aware training with 8-bit weights and 32-bit activations
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, but may require significant modifications to accommodate mobile or laptop hardware constraints.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
